issues.name: post-charge-crash-safety
issues.description: Ensure process never crashes after charging a card - user must always get success response. Implements durable payment flow.

context.scenario: User checkout → NMI.RunSale() succeeds (card charged) → subsequent step fails/panics → user sees error
context.problem: If we charge a card and then crash before returning success, the user sees an error but their card was charged. This is catastrophic UX and could cause support issues, chargebacks, or lost trust.
context.critical_window: Between NMI.RunSale() returning success and HTTP 200 being sent to user

durable_payment_flow.description: Job-based durable pattern - when post-charge ops fail, enqueue a retry job
steps: 1. Charge card → If fails, return error (safe to retry)
2. Record payment → Create Payment record with all context
3. Try post-charge operation (grant entitlements, renew membership, etc.)
4. If step 3 fails → Enqueue FulfillPaymentJob with all context needed to retry
5. Return success → User sees success, won't retry
6. FulfillPaymentWorker picks up job and retries the failed operation
why_not_scanning: Scanning workers (check DB for inconsistent state) are inefficient and implicit
Job-based approach is explicit - we know exactly what failed and what to retry
More efficient - no periodic DB scans, just process the queue
More auditable - job history shows exactly what failed and when

done: processNMISale(): After successful charge, return success even if RegisterPurchase fails
processUpgrade(): After successful proration charge, return success even if subsequent steps fail
jobs_dunning.go: Create Payment record BEFORE RenewMembership, continue even if either fails
Created internal/jobs package to avoid import cycles
Update processNMISale to enqueue FulfillPaymentJob on RegisterPurchase failure
Update processUpgrade to enqueue FulfillPaymentJob on post-charge failures
Update jobs_dunning.go to enqueue FulfillPaymentJob on RenewMembership failure
Remove old ReconcilePaymentsWorker periodic job from river_register.go

fulfillment_types: create_subscription - for upgrade scenarios (complex, may need manual review)

job_args.original_error: the error that caused the initial failure

issues.completed_notes: Fully implemented durable payment flow. When a card is charged but post-charge operations fail: (1) Payment record is created, (2) FulfillPaymentJob is enqueued via River for background retry, (3) Success is returned to user. FulfillPaymentWorker handles grant_entitlements, renew_membership, and create_subscription fulfillment types. Uses LazyFulfillmentEnqueuer pattern to handle River client initialization timing.

issues.name: nmi-test-account-integration
issues.description: Set up real Mobius/NMI test account for integration tests that hit the NMI API
steps: CURRENT: Tests use public NMI demo key which rejects operations on non-existent subscriptions
TARGET: Use real Mobius test account so we can create/cancel real test subscriptions
Obtain test credentials from Mobius (security_key, api_key)
Update testcontainer_suite.go NMI config with Mobius test credentials
Tests can then create real subscriptions via webhook simulation and cancel them via API
BENEFIT: Full integration testing of NMI flows with real data
tests_blocked: TestCancelSubscriptionNMI - tries to cancel non-existent subscription
TestCancelSubscriptionEmptyFeedback - tries to cancel non-existent subscription
TestSubscribeNMISuccess - requires valid NMI subscription creation

issues.description: Allow users to change which stored payment method their NMI subscription uses
steps: [DONE] NMI Client:
[DONE]   - Added UpdateSubscriptionPaymentSource(subscriptionID, customerVaultID string) to internal/integrations/nmi/nmi.go
[DONE]   - NMI API: recurring=update_subscription, subscription_id=X, customer_vault_id=Y
[DONE] Handler:
[DONE]   - Added PUT /v1/me/subscriptions/payment-method endpoint
[DONE]   - Request: { subscription_id: 'uuid', payment_method_id: 'uuid' }
[DONE]   - Validates user owns the subscription (from JWT)
[DONE]   - Validates user owns the target payment method
[DONE]   - Validates payment method is active and NMI-backed
[DONE]   - Validates subscription is NMI-backed (not CCBill/Solana)
[DONE]   - Validates subscription is active or past_due (not cancelled)
[DONE]   - Calls NMI to update subscription's customer_vault_id
[DONE]   - Updates local Subscription.PaymentMethodID in database
[DONE]   - Returns success response with subscription and payment method IDs

issues.completed_notes: Implemented PUT /v1/me/subscriptions/payment-method endpoint allowing users to update which stored NMI payment method (customer vault) their subscription uses. Handler validates ownership of both subscription and payment method, checks that both are NMI-backed and active, calls NMI API to update, and updates local DB. Also allows updating past_due subscriptions which is useful for fixing failed payment methods. Tests cover all validation scenarios.

[DONE]   - If BillingCycleDays != nil → subscription flow (see deduplication below)

issues.name: billing-consistency-audit
issues.description: CLI command to detect all possible inconsistencies in the billing system state

database_constraints.description: Database-level constraints that PREVENT inconsistencies from occurring
existing_constraints: UNIQUE(user_id) WHERE status = 'active' on subscriptions - only one active sub per user
UNIQUE(processor, transaction_id) on payments - no duplicate payment records
UNIQUE(user_id, entitlement) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND end_at IS NULL on entitlements - one indefinite entitlement per name
UNIQUE(processor, vault_id) on payment_methods - one vault per processor

recommended_new_constraints.constraint: UNIQUE(user_id, product_id) WHERE status IN ('active', 'pending') on subscriptions
recommended_new_constraints.purpose: Prevent multiple active/pending subscriptions for same product (currently only checks user_id)
recommended_new_constraints.migration: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_subscriptions_user_product_active ON billing.subscriptions(user_id, product_id) WHERE status IN ('active', 'pending')

recommended_new_constraints.constraint: CHECK (cancelled_at IS NOT NULL) WHERE status = 'cancelled' on subscriptions
recommended_new_constraints.purpose: Ensure cancelled subscriptions always have cancellation timestamp
recommended_new_constraints.migration: ALTER TABLE billing.subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT chk_cancelled_has_timestamp CHECK (status != 'cancelled' OR cancelled_at IS NOT NULL)

recommended_new_constraints.constraint: CHECK (cancel_type IS NOT NULL) WHERE status = 'cancelled' on subscriptions
recommended_new_constraints.purpose: Ensure cancelled subscriptions always have cancel reason
recommended_new_constraints.migration: ALTER TABLE billing.subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT chk_cancelled_has_type CHECK (status != 'cancelled' OR cancel_type IS NOT NULL)

recommended_new_constraints.constraint: CHECK (start_at < end_at) WHERE end_at IS NOT NULL on entitlements
recommended_new_constraints.purpose: Ensure entitlement time windows are valid
recommended_new_constraints.migration: ALTER TABLE billing.entitlements ADD CONSTRAINT chk_valid_time_window CHECK (end_at IS NULL OR start_at < end_at)

recommended_new_constraints.constraint: CHECK (revoked_at IS NOT NULL = revoke_reason IS NOT NULL) on entitlements
recommended_new_constraints.purpose: Ensure revoked_at and revoke_reason are always set together
recommended_new_constraints.migration: ALTER TABLE billing.entitlements ADD CONSTRAINT chk_revoke_fields_together CHECK ((revoked_at IS NULL) = (revoke_reason IS NULL))

recommended_new_constraints.purpose: Ensure subscription period dates are valid
recommended_new_constraints.migration: ALTER TABLE billing.subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT chk_valid_period CHECK (current_period_starts_at IS NULL OR current_period_ends_at IS NULL OR current_period_starts_at < current_period_ends_at)

recommended_new_constraints.constraint: EXCLUDE USING gist (user_id WITH =, entitlement WITH =, period WITH &&) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL on entitlements
recommended_new_constraints.purpose: Prevent overlapping entitlement windows for same user/entitlement (requires btree_gist extension)
recommended_new_constraints.note: Complex - may need application-level enforcement instead

examples: Entitlement source_id references subscription that exists (polymorphic FK)
constraints_not_possible.solution: Must be enforced via application logic or triggers

examples: Active subscription should have entitlements matching product spec
constraints_not_possible.solution: Must be checked via CLI audit tool

subscription_entitlement_mismatches.name: active_subscription_missing_entitlements
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.description: User has status=active subscription but is missing one or more entitlements from product's entitlements_spec
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.query_logic: For each active subscription: get product.entitlements_spec, check if user has active entitlement for each name with source_type=subscription and source_id=subscription.id

subscription_entitlement_mismatches.name: orphan_subscription_entitlements
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.description: User has entitlements with source_type=subscription but referenced subscription is cancelled/expired/missing
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.query_logic: Find entitlements WHERE source_type=subscription AND revoked_at IS NULL AND (source_id not in subscriptions OR subscription.status NOT IN (active, pending, past_due))

subscription_entitlement_mismatches.name: cancelled_subscription_active_entitlements
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.description: Subscription cancelled with revoke_access=true but entitlements still active (revoked_at IS NULL)
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.query_logic: Find subscriptions WHERE status=cancelled AND ended_at IS NOT NULL (immediate revoke) but entitlements with source_id=subscription.id still have revoked_at IS NULL AND end_at > now

subscription_entitlement_mismatches.name: wrong_entitlement_end_date
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.description: Subscription cancelled at period-end but entitlement end_at doesn't match current_period_ends_at
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.query_logic: Find subscriptions WHERE status=cancelled AND cancelled_at IS NOT NULL AND ended_at IS NULL (period-end cancel) but entitlements have end_at != subscription.current_period_ends_at

subscription_entitlement_mismatches.name: entitlement_source_mismatch
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.description: Entitlement's source_id references subscription that doesn't exist or belongs to different user
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.query_logic: Find entitlements WHERE source_type=subscription AND (source_id NOT IN subscriptions.id OR subscription.user_id != entitlement.user_id)
subscription_entitlement_mismatches.auto_fix: MANUAL - Flag for review, data corruption

payment_entitlement_mismatches.name: completed_payment_missing_entitlements
payment_entitlement_mismatches.description: Payment with status=completed and subscription_id=NULL (one-off) but no corresponding entitlements granted
payment_entitlement_mismatches.query_logic: Find payments WHERE status=completed AND subscription_id IS NULL but no entitlements exist with source_type=one_off AND source_id=payment.id

payment_entitlement_mismatches.name: orphan_one_off_entitlements
payment_entitlement_mismatches.description: Entitlements with source_type=one_off but referenced payment doesn't exist, was refunded, or failed
payment_entitlement_mismatches.query_logic: Find entitlements WHERE source_type=one_off AND revoked_at IS NULL AND (source_id NOT IN payments.id OR payment.status IN (refunded, failed))

payment_entitlement_mismatches.name: refunded_payment_active_entitlements
payment_entitlement_mismatches.description: Payment was fully refunded but entitlements still active
payment_entitlement_mismatches.query_logic: Find payments with refund records summing to >= original amount, but entitlements with source_id=payment.id still active

duplicate_issues.name: multiple_active_subscriptions
duplicate_issues.description: User has more than one subscription with status=active
duplicate_issues.query_logic: SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status='active' GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
duplicate_issues.auto_fix: MANUAL - Cancel older subscription, keep most recent

duplicate_issues.name: duplicate_charges_same_period
duplicate_issues.description: User charged twice for the same product in the same billing period
duplicate_issues.query_logic: Find payments for same user_id, same price.product_id, within 30-day window, both completed

duplicate_issues.name: overlapping_entitlement_windows
duplicate_issues.description: Same user has overlapping [start_at, end_at) windows for the same entitlement name
duplicate_issues.query_logic: Find entitlements WHERE user_id=X AND entitlement=Y AND revoked_at IS NULL with overlapping tstzrange(start_at, end_at)

subscription_state_issues.name: active_subscription_past_period_end
subscription_state_issues.description: status=active but current_period_ends_at < now() - should be past_due or cancelled
subscription_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE status='active' AND current_period_ends_at < NOW()
subscription_state_issues.auto_fix: Transition to past_due and attempt rebill, or cancel if grace period exceeded

subscription_state_issues.name: cancelled_without_metadata
subscription_state_issues.description: status=cancelled but cancelled_at IS NULL or cancel_type IS NULL
subscription_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE status='cancelled' AND (cancelled_at IS NULL OR cancel_type IS NULL)

subscription_state_issues.name: past_due_without_retry
subscription_state_issues.description: status=past_due but next_retry_at IS NULL and retry_attempts < max_retries
subscription_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE status='past_due' AND next_retry_at IS NULL AND retry_attempts < 5
subscription_state_issues.auto_fix: Set next_retry_at to schedule next dunning attempt

subscription_state_issues.name: invalid_period_dates
subscription_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE current_period_starts_at >= current_period_ends_at
subscription_state_issues.auto_fix: MANUAL - Flag for review, data corruption

subscription_state_issues.name: ended_before_cancelled
subscription_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE ended_at IS NOT NULL AND cancelled_at IS NOT NULL AND ended_at < cancelled_at

entitlement_state_issues.name: revoked_without_reason
entitlement_state_issues.description: revoked_at IS NOT NULL but revoke_reason IS NULL
entitlement_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM entitlements WHERE revoked_at IS NOT NULL AND revoke_reason IS NULL

entitlement_state_issues.name: reason_without_revocation
entitlement_state_issues.description: revoke_reason IS NOT NULL but revoked_at IS NULL
entitlement_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM entitlements WHERE revoke_reason IS NOT NULL AND revoked_at IS NULL

entitlement_state_issues.name: invalid_time_window
entitlement_state_issues.description: start_at >= end_at (when end_at is not null)
entitlement_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM entitlements WHERE end_at IS NOT NULL AND start_at >= end_at
entitlement_state_issues.auto_fix: MANUAL - Flag for review, data corruption

entitlement_state_issues.name: future_start_without_context
entitlement_state_issues.description: start_at > now() + 30 days without scheduled grant reason (suspicious)
entitlement_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM entitlements WHERE start_at > NOW() + INTERVAL '30 days'
entitlement_state_issues.auto_fix: Review - may be valid scheduled entitlement or data error

entitlement_state_issues.name: multiple_indefinite_entitlements
entitlement_state_issues.description: Multiple rows for same (user_id, entitlement) with end_at IS NULL AND revoked_at IS NULL
entitlement_state_issues.query_logic: SELECT user_id, entitlement, COUNT(*) FROM entitlements WHERE end_at IS NULL AND revoked_at IS NULL GROUP BY user_id, entitlement HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
entitlement_state_issues.auto_fix: Revoke all but the most recent

payment_method_issues.name: active_subscription_inactive_payment_method
payment_method_issues.query_logic: SELECT s.* FROM subscriptions s JOIN payment_methods pm ON s.payment_method_id = pm.id WHERE s.status='active' AND pm.is_active=false
payment_method_issues.auto_fix: Prompt user to update payment method, or transition to past_due

payment_method_issues.name: expired_card_active_subscription
payment_method_issues.description: Payment method expiry_date < now but subscription still active
payment_method_issues.query_logic: SELECT s.* FROM subscriptions s JOIN payment_methods pm ON s.payment_method_id = pm.id WHERE s.status='active' AND pm.expiry_date < TO_CHAR(NOW(), 'MM/YY')
payment_method_issues.auto_fix: Notify user to update payment method before next rebill fails

payment_method_issues.name: orphan_payment_method_reference
payment_method_issues.description: Subscription references payment_method_id that doesn't exist
payment_method_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE payment_method_id IS NOT NULL AND payment_method_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM payment_methods)

payment_method_issues.name: processor_mismatch
payment_method_issues.description: Subscription processor doesn't match payment method processor
payment_method_issues.query_logic: SELECT s.* FROM subscriptions s JOIN payment_methods pm ON s.payment_method_id = pm.id WHERE s.processor != pm.processor
payment_method_issues.auto_fix: MANUAL - Flag for review, configuration error

foreign_key_issues.name: orphan_subscription_product
foreign_key_issues.description: Subscription references product_id that doesn't exist or is inactive
foreign_key_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM products WHERE is_active=true)

foreign_key_issues.name: orphan_subscription_price
foreign_key_issues.description: Subscription references price_id that doesn't exist or is inactive
foreign_key_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE price_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM prices WHERE is_active=true)

foreign_key_issues.name: price_product_mismatch
foreign_key_issues.description: Subscription's price.product_id doesn't match subscription's product_id
foreign_key_issues.query_logic: SELECT s.* FROM subscriptions s JOIN prices p ON s.price_id = p.id WHERE s.product_id != p.product_id

foreign_key_issues.name: payment_orphan_subscription
foreign_key_issues.description: Payment has subscription_id that doesn't exist
foreign_key_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM payments WHERE subscription_id IS NOT NULL AND subscription_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM subscriptions)
foreign_key_issues.auto_fix: Clear subscription_id (payment record is still valid)

foreign_key_issues.name: entitlement_orphan_source
foreign_key_issues.query_logic: Find entitlements where source_type=subscription but source_id not in subscriptions, OR source_type=one_off but source_id not in payments
foreign_key_issues.auto_fix: MANUAL - Flag for review, may be valid historical entitlement

admin_grant_issues.name: admin_grant_missing_entitlements
admin_grant_issues.description: admin_grants record exists but no corresponding entitlements with source_type=admin
admin_grant_issues.query_logic: Find admin_grants where no entitlements exist with source_type=admin for same user and product entitlements

admin_grant_issues.name: admin_grant_payment_mismatch
admin_grant_issues.description: Admin grant references payment_id that doesn't exist or has wrong user
admin_grant_issues.query_logic: SELECT ag.* FROM admin_grants ag WHERE ag.payment_id IS NOT NULL AND (ag.payment_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM payments) OR ag.user_id != (SELECT user_id FROM payments WHERE id = ag.payment_id))

temporal_issues.name: stale_pending_subscription
temporal_issues.description: status=pending for more than 24 hours AND current_period_starts_at <= now() (should have activated or failed)
temporal_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE status='pending' AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours' AND current_period_starts_at <= NOW()
temporal_issues.auto_fix: Check with processor, then cancel or activate

temporal_issues.name: stale_past_due_max_retries
temporal_issues.description: status=past_due with retry_attempts >= 5 but not transitioned to cancelled
temporal_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE status='past_due' AND retry_attempts >= 5

temporal_issues.name: expired_entitlement_not_cleaned
temporal_issues.description: Entitlement with end_at < now() but revoked_at IS NULL (technically expired but not marked)
temporal_issues.query_logic: SELECT * FROM entitlements WHERE end_at < NOW() AND revoked_at IS NULL
temporal_issues.auto_fix: No fix needed - query logic handles this, but tracks for cleanup stats

subcommands: billing-cli audit all - run all consistency checks
billing-cli audit subscriptions - subscription-related checks (S-E-*, SS-*)
billing-cli audit entitlements - entitlement-related checks (ES-*, S-E-*, P-E-*)
billing-cli audit payments - payment-related checks (P-E-*, FK-4)
billing-cli audit foreign-keys - foreign key integrity (FK-*)
billing-cli audit payment-methods - payment method issues (PM-*)
flags: --fix - attempt automatic fixes where safe (with confirmation prompt)
--dry-run - show what would be fixed without making changes (default)
--since=DATE - only check records created after date
--quiet - only output issues found, no progress

summary_output.description: After running checks, show summary stats
fields: Issues by severity (critical: X, high: Y, ...)

implementation_steps: Create cmd/billing-cli/main.go with cobra CLI framework
Add database connection using existing config loading
Create internal/audit package with AuditCheck interface and AuditFinding struct
Implement each check category as separate file (audit_subscriptions.go, audit_entitlements.go, etc.)
Each check returns []AuditFinding with ID, severity, entity info, description, recommendation
Add --format output formatters (table using tablewriter, json, csv)
Add --fix handlers for auto-fixable issues with confirmation prompts
Add --severity filtering and --user filtering
Add summary statistics output
Write tests with seeded inconsistent data for each check
Create migration for recommended database constraints (C-1 through C-6)
Document all checks and their fixes in README
issues.completed_notes: Implemented CLI audit command with 32 consistency checks across 9 categories. Run with: `./doujins-billing audit`. Supports --format (table/json/csv), --user-id, --severity, --category flags. Checks include subscription-entitlement sync, payment-entitlement sync, duplicate detection, state validation, payment method issues, foreign key integrity, admin grant consistency, and temporal issues. Output shows severity-colored findings with recommendations and auto-fix indicators.

issues.name: processor-sync
issues.description: CLI tool to detect and fix discrepancies between local database state and external processor state (NMI/Mobius, CCBill)

context.problem: Our database may drift out of sync with processor records due to missed webhooks, failed processing, network issues, or bugs

data_sources.ccbill: DataLink API - provides batch export of subscription and transaction data
data_sources.nmi: Transaction Query API and Customer Vault Query API - query transactions and stored payment methods

use_cases: Detect subscriptions in processor that we don't have locally
Detect subscriptions we have locally that processor cancelled/expired
Find payments we missed (charged but no Payment record)

ccbill_datalink.description: CCBill DataLink provides SFTP/API access to batch export files
data_available: Subscription transactions (new, rebill, cancel, expire, chargeback)
sync_flow: 1. Fetch DataLink export for date range
3. For each transaction, check if we have matching record
ccbill_datalink.existing_code: internal/integrations/ccbill/datalink.go exists but may be incomplete

nmi_batch_api.description: NMI Query API allows transaction and subscription lookup
endpoints: GET transactions - query by date range, customer, subscription
GET customers - query customer vault records
sync_flow: 1. Query NMI for transactions in date range

discrepancy_types.name: processor_subscription_not_local
discrepancy_types.description: Subscription exists at processor but not in our database
discrepancy_types.action: Create local subscription record or investigate orphan at processor

discrepancy_types.name: local_subscription_not_at_processor
discrepancy_types.description: We have an active subscription but processor shows cancelled/expired
discrepancy_types.action: Update local status to match processor

discrepancy_types.name: status_mismatch
discrepancy_types.description: Local status differs from processor status (e.g., we show active, they show past_due)
discrepancy_types.action: Reconcile to processor's status (source of truth)

discrepancy_types.name: payment_missing_locally
discrepancy_types.description: Processor has successful charge but no Payment record locally
discrepancy_types.action: Create Payment record and grant entitlements if needed

discrepancy_types.name: refund_not_processed
discrepancy_types.description: Processor shows refund but no refund record locally
discrepancy_types.action: Create refund record, revoke entitlements if appropriate

discrepancy_types.name: chargeback_not_processed
discrepancy_types.description: Processor shows chargeback but subscription still active locally

discrepancy_types.name: payment_method_vault_mismatch
discrepancy_types.description: Local payment method record differs from processor vault (wrong card, inactive)
discrepancy_types.action: Update local record to match processor

subcommands: billing-cli processor-sync check - find discrepancies (dry run)
billing-cli processor-sync fix - find and fix discrepancies
billing-cli processor-sync report - generate detailed report
flags: --dry-run - show what would be fixed without making changes

auto_fixable: PS-2: Update local status to match processor
PS-3: Update local status to match processor
PS-7: Update local payment method record
requires_manual_review: PS-1: Orphan processor subscription needs investigation
PS-4: Missing payment needs entitlement check
PS-5: Refund may need entitlement revocation decision

Implement NMISyncer that queries NMI API and compares to local

issues.name: admin-user-search
issues.description: Sophisticated user search for admin dashboard
steps: Design search API:
- GET /v1/admin/users?q=...&filters... - search users with billing data
Search fields:
- email (partial match from subscriptions/payments)
- user_id (exact match)
- processor_subscription_id (exact match for support lookups)
- transaction_id (exact match for payment support)
Filters:
- has_subscription=true/false
- subscription_status=active/cancelled/past_due/etc
- processor=nmi/ccbill/solana
- has_entitlement=premium/etc
- created_after, created_before (date range)
Sorting:
- sort_by=newest/oldest/last_payment/subscription_start
Pagination:
- page, page_size, cursor-based pagination for large results
Performance:
- Add database indexes for search fields
- Consider full-text search for email
- Rate limit search queries
NOTE: Billing service searches its own data. Full user profile search (username, etc) lives in main doujins API.

issues.name: admin-rate-limiting
issues.description: Add rate limiting to admin endpoints to limit blast radius of compromised JWT
steps: PROBLEM: If admin JWT is leaked, attacker could cancel/refund thousands of users before detection
Implement per-admin-user rate limiting using Redis (keyed by admin user ID from JWT)
Define rate limits for destructive operations: e.g., 5 cancellations/minute, 10/hour, 50/day
Define rate limits for bulk operations: e.g., 3 extend operations/minute
On rate limit exceeded: lock out admin for extended period (e.g., 1 hour) and alert
Add alerting/notification when rate limits are approached (e.g., 80% threshold)
Create admin rate limit middleware that wraps destructive endpoints
Allow super-admin or manual override to unlock rate-limited admin if legitimate
Log all rate limit events for security audit
BENEFIT: Limits blast radius - attacker can only affect ~5-10 users before getting locked out

issues.name: solana-pay-transaction-request
issues.description: Implement Solana Pay Transaction Request spec (server-built transactions via HTTPS endpoint)
steps: CURRENT: We only support Transfer Requests (solana:<recipient>?amount=X) where wallet builds the transaction
TARGET: Also support Transaction Requests (solana:https://api.example.com/pay) where server builds the transaction
Create GET /v1/solana-pay/:intent_id endpoint returning { label, icon } per spec
Create POST /v1/solana-pay/:intent_id endpoint accepting { account } and returning { transaction, message? }
Server builds the transaction with the user's account as signer (not fee payer if we want sponsored txs)
Return base64-encoded serialized transaction for wallet to sign and broadcast
Update CreatePaymentIntentQR to optionally return transaction request URL instead of transfer request URL
Add config option to choose between transfer vs transaction request mode
BENEFIT: Enables complex transactions (swaps, multi-instruction), sponsored fees, and server-side transaction building
Write tests for transaction request flow

issues.name: admin-grants-and-offline-purchases
issues.description: Admin grants products to users (comps, contest winners, manual payments) via dedicated AdminGrant table

design.concept: Admin grants work like user purchases - admin picks a Price/Product, system derives entitlements from Product.EntitlementsSpec

AdminGrant.granted_by: string - admin user ID who made the grant
AdminGrant.payment_id: uuid nullable - only if money was received (links to Payment record)
AdminGrant.duration_days: int nullable - override entitlement duration (null = use Product.EntitlementsSpec)

free_comp: 2. Grant entitlements from Product.EntitlementsSpec (with optional duration override)
manual_payment: 1. Create Payment record with processor='admin', transaction_id (optional external ref)
2. Create AdminGrant record with payment_id pointing to Payment

request.reason: required - arbitrary string explaining the grant
request.amount: optional - if > 0, creates Payment record too
request.transaction_id: optional - external reference (PayPal ID, cash receipt, etc.)

issues.name: credits-balance-system
issues.description: Implement a credits/balance system for consumable purchases (API credits, GPU time, AI tokens)

key_concepts: Each currency = one wallet. Multiple currencies = multiple wallets per user.
Sub-wallets per platform source (Steam, PlayStation, System) - balances sum together
Two balance types: Permanent (never expires) and Time-Limited (expires after set date)
Time-limited balances deplete BEFORE permanent, regardless of source priority
Japan region: 180-day expiration required for paid virtual currency (Payment Services Act)
Credit operations track: amount, source type (Purchase, Promotion, Achievement, Referral_Bonus, Redeem_Code, Other), optional expiration
Full transaction history available for audit
Currency defined as catalog item with FriendlyId (1-3 char code)
Legacy v1 had 'recharge rate' - auto-grant X credits per day (interesting for daily allowances)
Currencies tied to catalog items for purchasing
Less sophisticated than AccelByte - no expiration, no sub-wallets

design_decisions.terminology: Use 'credits' as the generic term. Each credit type is a 'CreditType' (e.g., 'gpu_time', 'api_calls', 'ai_tokens')
design_decisions.expiration: Support optional expiration per credit grant (like AccelByte time-limited balance)
design_decisions.fifo_expiration: Expiring credits consumed first, then permanent credits (AccelByte pattern)
design_decisions.no_sub_wallets: Skip sub-wallets complexity - we don't have multi-platform sources like gaming
design_decisions.ledger_based: Append-only transaction ledger for audit trail, with denormalized balance for fast reads

columns: display_name TEXT NOT NULL -- 'GPU Minutes', 'API Credits'

user_credit_balances.description: Current balance per user per credit type (denormalized for fast reads)
columns: balance BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 -- total available (permanent + unexpired)
permanent_balance BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 -- never expires
expiring_balance BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 -- sum of unexpired time-limited
earliest_expiry TIMESTAMPTZ -- when next credits expire (for efficient expiry job)

credit_transactions.description: Append-only ledger of all credit changes
columns: amount BIGINT NOT NULL -- positive = deposit, negative = withdrawal
balance_after BIGINT NOT NULL -- balance after this transaction
expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ -- for deposits: when these credits expire (NULL = permanent)

credit_expiry_batches.description: Tracks expiring credit batches for FIFO consumption
columns: INDEX(user_id, credit_type_id, expires_at ASC) -- for FIFO consumption

purchase_flow: RegisterPurchase() calls CreditService.Deposit() for each credit type
Deposit creates transaction + updates balance + optionally creates expiry batch
product_integration.expiration_from_product: Product could specify credits_expiry_days (e.g., 90 days). If set, credits expire 90 days from purchase.

CreditService.Deposit(ctx, userID, creditType, amount,: Add credits, create transaction, update balance
CreditService.Withdraw(ctx, userID, creditType, amount: Deduct credits (FIFO: expiring first), returns ErrInsufficientBalance if not enough
CreditService.ExpireCredits(ctx): Background job to expire old credits and update balances

withdrawal_fifo_logic: 1. Get all unexpired expiry batches for user+creditType, ordered by expires_at ASC
3. If batch depleted, move to next batch
4. After all expiring batches, deduct from permanent balance
6. Create transaction record with amount, balance_after

logic: Find all expiry_batches WHERE expires_at <= NOW() AND remaining_amount > 0
For each: create 'expiry' transaction, set remaining_amount = 0

user_facing: GET /v1/me/credits -- list all credit balances for user
GET /v1/me/credits/:type -- get detailed balance for specific credit type
internal: POST /v1/internal/credits/withdraw -- called by API/GPU service to deduct credits

usage_integration.pattern: External services (API gateway, GPU scheduler) call internal endpoint to withdraw credits before allowing operation
example_flow: User requests GPU job costing 10 credits
GPU service calls POST /v1/internal/credits/withdraw {user_id, credit_type: 'gpu_time', amount: 10, source: 'gpu_job', source_id: job_id}
If success: GPU service proceeds with job
If ErrInsufficientBalance: GPU service rejects with 402 Payment Required

implementation_steps: Create migration for credit_types, user_credit_balances, credit_transactions, credit_expiry_batches tables
Create CreditType model
Create UserCreditBalance model
Create CreditTransaction model
Create CreditExpiryBatch model
Create CreditTypeRepo with CRUD operations
Create CreditBalanceRepo with balance operations
Create CreditTransactionRepo for ledger operations
Create CreditService with Deposit/Withdraw/GetBalance logic
Implement FIFO withdrawal logic with expiry batch consumption
Add CreditsSpec field to Product model
Update RegisterPurchase to grant credits from Product.CreditsSpec
Create River job for credit expiry processing
Create GET /v1/me/credits endpoint
Create GET /v1/me/credits/:type endpoint
Create GET /v1/me/credits/:type/transactions endpoint
Create POST /v1/admin/users/:id/credits endpoint
Create POST /v1/internal/credits/withdraw endpoint
Write tests for deposit/withdraw/expiry flows
Write tests for FIFO consumption order
Document credit system in API docs

issues.name: service-api-endpoints
issues.description: Add X-API-Key authenticated endpoints for server-to-server calls (separate from public/admin)

context.problem: AuthKit and other backend services need to query billing data programmatically without a user JWT
use_cases: AuthKit needs to fetch user entitlements during JWT token issuance (no user JWT available yet)
Backend services may need to check subscription status server-to-server
Future: inter-service communication for credits, usage tracking, etc.
context.solution: Keep a separate private API endpoint (port 8060) with X-API-Key authentication for trusted server-to-server calls

design.authentication: X-API-Key header with shared secret (configured via BILLING_API_KEY env var)
design.endpoint_location: Private port (8060) - NOT exposed to public internet, only internal Docker network

endpoints.description: Get user's active entitlements (for AuthKit token enrichment)
Check if user has active subscription

security_notes: Private port should NEVER be exposed to public internet
API key should be long, random, and rotated periodically
Consider IP allowlisting in production (only allow from known service IPs)
Log all service API calls for audit
Rate limit to prevent abuse even from internal services

[DONE] Implement GET /v1/service/users/:user_id/entitlements handler
[DONE] Implement GET /v1/service/users/:user_id/subscription-status handler
[DONE] Add APIKey config field and PrivatePort (default 8060) to config.go
[DONE] Add APIKeyRequired middleware with constant-time comparison
[DONE] Update main.go to start both public and private servers
[DONE] Update docker-compose to expose port 8060 only internally (no host binding)
[DONE] Update doujins ServiceClient to use /v1/service routes
[DONE] Update doujins docker-compose with BILLING_ADMIN_URL and BILLING_API_KEY
[DONE] Add GET /v1/admin/users/:user_id/entitlements for admin dashboard use
[N/A] hentai0 doesn't need service API - uses JWT auth for admin endpoints
issues.completed_notes: Implemented private service API on port 8060 with X-API-KEY auth. Added GET /v1/service/users/:user_id/entitlements and GET /v1/service/users/:user_id/subscription-status for server-to-server calls. Also added GET /v1/admin/users/:user_id/entitlements for admin dashboard lookups via JWT auth. doujins ServiceClient updated to use the new /v1/service routes. Docker-compose files updated with BILLING_ADMIN_URL=http://billing:8060 and BILLING_API_KEY env vars.

issues.name: admin-dashboard-metrics-overhaul
issues.description: Overhaul admin dashboard metrics to provide useful business intelligence

metrics_provided: active_users_without_auto_renew - counts active subs where cancelled_at IS NOT NULL (confusing)
active_users_with_auto_renew - counts active subs for CCBill/NMI processors
current_state.processor_metrics: Same 3 metrics broken down by processor

problems: Limited metrics - only subscription counts, no revenue or business KPIs
Confusing naming - 'without_auto_renew' actually means 'cancelled but still in period'
No revenue metrics - MRR, ARR, total revenue, average revenue per user
No churn metrics - churn rate, retention rate, LTV
No Solana support - processor metrics exclude Solana one-time payments
No period comparisons - can't compare this week/month vs previous
No cohort analysis - can't track retention by signup month
No conversion funnel - signups to first payment to recurring
Tests are minimal - just verify response structure, not actual data accuracy

revenue_metrics: MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) - sum of active subscription monthly values
Total revenue this period - sum of all payments in date range
Revenue by processor - breakdown by CCBill, NMI, Solana
ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) - total revenue / active users
subscription_metrics: Cancelled subscriptions this period (with breakdown by reason)
churn_metrics: Monthly churn rate - cancelled / active at start of month
Retention rate by cohort - % of month-N signups still active

dashboard_summary.returns: Key KPIs for dashboard cards (MRR, active subs, churn rate, etc.)

churn_analysis.returns: Churn breakdown by reason, cohort retention

implementation_notes: MRR calculation: sum(price.amount / (price.billing_cycle_days / 30)) for active subs
Need to handle different billing cycles (monthly, quarterly, annual)
Solana one-time payments are not subscriptions - track separately
Consider caching expensive aggregations (refresh every 5 min)
Use PostgreSQL window functions for period-over-period comparisons
May want ClickHouse for historical analytics at scale

Implement churn rate calculation

issues.name: fix-nmi-add-subscription-missing-payment-info
issues.description: NMI handleAddSubscription() doesn't pass payment info to CreateMembership(), so no Payment record is created for initial subscriptions

context.problem: handleAddSubscription() calls CreateMembership() without TransactionID, Amount, or Currency - meaning new NMI subscriptions don't create Payment records
context.comparison: CCBill handleNewSaleSuccess() correctly passes all payment info to CreateMembership()
context.impact: Initial NMI subscription payments are not recorded in the payments table, breaking revenue tracking

steps: [DONE] Extract amount from body.Plan.Amount in handleAddSubscription()
[DONE] Use price.Currency or default to USD
[DONE] Use 'sub:' + subscriptionID as transaction reference (NMI doesn't include txn ID in subscription add events)
[DONE] Pass TransactionID, Amount, Currency to CreateMembership() in handleAddSubscription()
[DONE] Also fixed handleTransactionSaleSuccess() for pending subscriptions - was missing payment info there too
[NOTE] Tests already exist via webhook replay tests
issues.completed_notes: Fixed both handleAddSubscription() and handleTransactionSaleSuccess() to pass payment info to CreateMembership(). For subscription add events, amount comes from plan.amount (converted from dollars to cents), currency from price or default USD, and transaction ref uses 'sub:' prefix since NMI doesn't include transaction ID in subscription add webhooks. For transaction sale success with pending subscriptions, the actual transaction ID and amount are available and now passed through.

issues.name: add-nmi-refund-void-handlers
issues.description: Add NMI webhook handlers for refund and void events - NMI supports these but we don't handle them

context.ccbill_has: CCBill has handleRefund() and handleVoid() with subscription termination logic
context.current_state: NMI only handles transaction.sale.success/failure, recurring.*, acu.*, and chargeback.batch.complete

event_types_to_add: transaction.refund.success - Create refund payment record, optionally terminate subscription if >80% refunded
transaction.void.success - Log void event, update payment record if applicable
steps: [DONE] Add EventTypeNMIRefundSuccess and EventTypeNMIVoidSuccess constants
[DONE] Create handleRefundSuccess() handler matching CCBill's refund logic
[DONE] Create handleVoidSuccess() handler
[DONE] Add subscription termination logic for significant refunds (>=80%)
[DONE] Add event logging to ClickHouse for refund/void events

issues.name: add-nmi-chargeback-subscription-termination
issues.description: NMI chargeback handler only logs batch events - should terminate subscriptions and revoke entitlements like CCBill does

context.current_behavior: handleChargebackComplete() only logs batch-level chargeback data to ClickHouse
context.ccbill_behavior: handleChargeback() terminates subscription immediately and revokes all entitlements
context.problem: NMI chargebacks don't trigger any subscription state changes
context.nmi_limitation: NMI chargeback webhooks are batch-based and do NOT include transaction_id or subscription info, making automatic subscription termination impossible without additional API lookups

steps: [DONE] Parse individual chargeback records from NMI batch webhook
[DONE] Add NMI chargeback batch types (NMIChargebackBatchEventBody, NMIChargebackEntry, etc.)
[DONE] Log individual chargeback events to ClickHouse with requires_manual_review flag
[N/A] Automatic subscription termination NOT POSSIBLE - NMI doesn't provide subscription linkage in chargeback data (unlike CCBill)
[NOTE] Manual review required to match chargebacks to subscriptions via cc_last4, customer_name, and amount
issues.completed_notes: NMI chargeback handler now parses batch data and logs individual chargebacks to ClickHouse. Automatic subscription termination is not possible because NMI doesn't include transaction_id or subscription_id in chargeback webhooks (unlike CCBill). Each chargeback is logged with requires_manual_review=true flag.

issues.description: NMI doesn't log subscription lifecycle events to ClickHouse - CCBill logs create/cancel/expire/reactivate events

ccbill_logs: billing_date_changed - when billing date is modified

issues.impact: Can't analyze subscription lifecycle metrics for NMI subscriptions in ClickHouse

issues.name: prevent-duplicate-subscriptions
issues.description: Fix race conditions and logic gaps that could allow duplicate active subscriptions for the same user/product

context.problem: Multiple code paths can create subscriptions, and application-level checks have race conditions that could allow duplicates
context.impact: User could be charged twice for overlapping subscription periods - support nightmare, chargebacks, lost trust

issues_identified.title: CreateMembership only checks most recent subscription
issues_identified.description: GetLatestByUserID returns only the most recent subscription by created_at. If user has cancelled Sub A and active Sub B, and GetLatestByUserID returns Sub A (cancelled), the check passes and creates a THIRD subscription.
issues_identified.fix: Use GetActiveSubscription or query for ANY active subscription, not just the latest

issues_identified.title: No database-level uniqueness constraint on recurring subscriptions
issues_identified.description: No unique index prevents (user_id + status='active' + product_id) for recurring subscriptions. Note: this only applies to recurring products - one-time purchases don't create subscription records (they create Payment + Entitlement directly). For recurring subscriptions, two concurrent requests can both pass application-level checks and both INSERT.
issues_identified.fix: Add partial unique index: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ON subscriptions(user_id, product_id) WHERE status IN ('active', 'pending'). This constraint only affects recurring subscriptions, which is the correct behavior.

issues_identified.title: FulfillPaymentWorker can create duplicate NMI subscriptions
issues_identified.description: If job runs twice (River retry), first run creates NMI subscription but fails before saving locally. Second run checks local DB (empty), creates ANOTHER NMI subscription. Two subscriptions at NMI both charging user.
issues_identified.fix: Query NMI by customerid+planid before creating new subscription, or use idempotency key with NMI

issues_identified.title: CCBill double-click creates multiple transactions
issues_identified.description: User clicks Pay, CCBill processes, webhook creates Sub A. User's browser slow, clicks Pay again. CCBill creates another transaction. Second webhook arrives - protected by CreateMembership check BUT see issue #1.

issues_identified.title: FulfillPaymentWorker race with webhooks
issues_identified.description: Checkout fails after NMI subscription created, job enqueued. Meanwhile webhook arrives and creates subscription. Job runs and might create duplicate if different product/tier.
issues_identified.mitigation: Already mitigated by tier group checks, but should add processor_subscription_id check

recommended_fixes.action: Add database partial unique index on (user_id, product_id) WHERE status IN ('active', 'pending')
FulfillPaymentWorker: Query NMI before creating subscription
Add processor_subscription_id uniqueness check in fulfillment worker

alternative_approach.name: Durable Execution Framework
alternative_approach.description: Current approach requires manual idempotency checks everywhere. Could use Temporal, Hatchet, or Restate for automatic durable execution guarantees.
options: Temporal - Full-featured, self-hosted or cloud, steep learning curve
Hatchet - Open source Temporal alternative, simpler
DBOS - Uses Postgres as durability layer, minimal infrastructure

issues.name: test-architecture-improvements
issues.description: Tests should use real structs and functions from production code, not invent test-specific abstractions

philosophy.principle: Tests should verify actual production code behavior, not test-specific wrappers
philosophy.goal: When production code changes, tests should break if they're testing the affected behavior
philosophy.anti_pattern: Test helper structs that diverge from production models hide API changes and create maintenance burden

current_problems: SubscriptionOptions helper struct uses different field names than models.Subscription (PeriodStart vs CurrentPeriodStartsAt)
Tests can pass while using wrong field names because helpers translate between them
When a model field is renamed/removed, tests may not break because helper abstracts it away
Developers get confused about which struct to use and what fields are available
recommendations: Use models.Subscription directly in tests instead of SubscriptionOptions
Use api.ListResponse[T] for parsing API responses instead of map[string]interface{}
Import and use the actual request/response structs from handlers
If a helper is needed, it should take the real model struct as input, not a parallel struct
Test assertions should use strongly-typed response parsing

example_good.benefit: Uses real model, compiler catches field name errors

refactoring_steps: Audit all test helper structs (SubscriptionOptions, PaymentMethodOptions, etc.)
Replace helper structs with direct model usage where possible
Use json.Unmarshal with actual API response types instead of map[string]interface{}
Add linter rule or CI check to discourage map[string]interface{} in tests

issues.name: durable-workflow-execution
issues.description: Evaluate and potentially adopt embedded durable workflow execution for critical payment flows

context.problem: Current approach requires manual idempotency checks scattered throughout codebase. Edge cases can slip through, and crash recovery depends on job queues plus manual retry logic.
context.goal: Workflows that are deterministic, automatically resume from crash point, and never double-execute external API calls
requirements: EMBEDDED library - no external containers/services to host
Record outcomes of external APIs to avoid re-execution

how_durable_execution_works.determinism: Workflow code must be deterministic - no random, no time.Now(), no map iteration, no select
how_durable_execution_works.event_sourcing: Every step's result is persisted to a log (event history)
how_durable_execution_works.replay: On crash/restart, replay events to rebuild state without re-executing side effects
how_durable_execution_works.activities: Side effects (API calls, DB writes) are wrapped as 'activities' - executed once, result cached
how_durable_execution_works.idempotency: Built-in - if activity already executed, cached result is returned on replay

pros: Can run worker in same process or separate
cons: Workflow code must follow determinism rules

durabletask_go.maturity: Work-in-progress, NOT production ready per Microsoft
pros: Backed by Microsoft, used in Azure Durable Functions
cons: SQLite only - would need to implement PostgreSQL backend

build_own.description: Create a lightweight durable execution library tailored to our needs
components: EventLog table - stores (workflow_id, step_id, step_type, input, output, created_at)
Activity wrapper - executes external calls, persists results
Replay logic - on resume, read event log, skip already-executed steps
Determinism checker - runtime validation that workflow is deterministic

schema.workflow_executions: (id, workflow_type, workflow_id, status, started_at, completed_at, error)
schema.workflow_events: (id, execution_id, sequence, event_type, step_name, input_json, output_json, created_at)

// If we crash after Step 2 but before Step 4:

candidate_workflows: Subscription upgrade (charge proration → cancel old → create new)
Refund flow (refund at processor → revoke entitlements → update records)

replaces_existing.description: Durable workflows will replace our ad-hoc error recovery system
code_to_remove: All EnqueueFulfillment() calls in checkout_service.go, jobs_dunning.go
why_replaced: FulfillPaymentWorker is a manual retry mechanism - workflows handle this automatically
LazyFulfillmentEnqueuer solves init ordering - workflows have their own lifecycle
Manual idempotency checks in worker - workflows cache activity results automatically
EnqueueFulfillment pattern - replaced by workflow.ExecuteActivity pattern

If adopting: Create workflow definitions for checkout, upgrade, dunning
If building: Design minimal event log schema and replay engine

issues.name: nmi-idempotency-keys
issues.description: Implement idempotency keys for NMI payment operations to prevent double-charging during retries

context.problem: When Temporal/durable workflows retry a failed step, we risk double-charging if the charge succeeded but the response was lost. NMI's classic transact.php API doesn't natively support idempotency keys.
context.goal: Ensure payment API calls are idempotent - retrying the same operation returns the cached result instead of charging again
context.defense_in_depth: Even with durable workflows caching activity results, API-level idempotency is a safety net for edge cases

nmi_sign_up_api.note: Used for merchant onboarding, not transaction processing

nmi_payment_api_transact_php.format: URL-encoded POST to transact.php, ampersand-delimited response
nmi_payment_api_transact_php.note: Classic Direct Post API - no native idempotency support

current_implementation.uses: transact.php Direct Post API with url.Values{} and http.PostForm()
current_implementation.no_idempotency: No idempotency keys sent with requests

implementation_options.name: Application-level idempotency table
implementation_options.description: Before calling NMI, check/insert idempotency key in DB. After success, store result. On retry, return cached result.
pros: Can store rich metadata about the operation
cons: Must handle race conditions (SELECT FOR UPDATE or UPSERT)
Duplication of durable workflow's activity caching

columns: operation_type VARCHAR NOT NULL (sale, refund, void, add_subscription)
request_hash VARCHAR (hash of request params for verification)

implementation_options.name: NMI order_id deduplication
implementation_options.description: NMI's order_id field may prevent duplicate transactions if same order_id is submitted twice
cons: Needs verification - unclear if NMI actually dedupes on order_id
May only work within a time window
implementation_options.investigation_needed: Test if NMI rejects duplicate order_id values or processes them as separate transactions

implementation_options.name: Rely solely on durable workflows
implementation_options.description: Durable workflows (go-workflows) cache activity results - if ChargeCardActivity succeeds, replay returns cached result
pros: Single source of truth for idempotency
cons: No defense-in-depth if workflow state is corrupted/lost
Workflow must be adopted first (separate task)
implementation_options.recommendation: Start here, add Option A only if edge cases emerge

recommended_approach.phase_1: Adopt durable workflows (separate task) - provides activity-level idempotency
recommended_approach.phase_2: Monitor for any double-charge incidents in production
recommended_approach.phase_3: If incidents occur, implement Option A (idempotency table) as defense-in-depth
recommended_approach.idempotency_key_format: checkout:{user_id}:{price_id}:{timestamp_minute} or use UUID generated by workflow

operations_needing_idempotency.risk: Create duplicate NMI subscriptions (unlikely to double-charge immediately but creates mess)
Double-refund (user gets too much money back)

issues.name: wrap-nmi-calls-with-idempotency
issues.description: Use existing IdempotencyService to wrap all NMI API calls, preventing double-charges on retry

context.problem: NMI's transact.php API has no native idempotency support. If a request succeeds but response is lost (network timeout, crash), retry will double-charge.
existing_infrastructure: idempotency_requests table with operation, key, status, result_json columns
Begin() / Complete() / Fail() pattern already implemented
context.gap: IdempotencyService is injected into CheckoutService but NOT actually used before NMI calls

implementation_pattern.description: Wrap each NMI call with idempotency check
pseudo_code: if req.Status == 'pending' { return errors.New('checkout in progress') }

time_bucket.description: Use minute bucket in checkout keys (e.g., 2025-12-07T15:30)
time_bucket.rationale: Rapid retries within same minute are deduplicated, but user can retry later if genuinely needed

pending_status_handling.description: If Begin() returns status=pending, another request is in flight
options: Return error 'checkout in progress, please wait' (recommended)

result_caching.description: Store full NMI response in result_json for replay

implementation_steps: Add GenerateForSale(userID, priceID, timestamp) to IdempotencyService
Add GenerateForSubscription(userID, priceID, timestamp) to IdempotencyService
Wrap processNMISale() with Begin/Complete/Fail pattern
Wrap processNMISubscription() with Begin/Complete/Fail pattern
Wrap jobs_dunning.go rebill with Begin/Complete/Fail pattern
Add integration tests for idempotency (retry same checkout, verify no double-charge)
Handle 'pending' status edge case (concurrent requests)
files_to_modify: internal/river/jobs_dunning.go - Wrap rebill charge with idempotency
issues.note: This can be implemented NOW without waiting for durable workflows. It uses existing infrastructure.

issues.name: direct-card-processing
issues.description: Accept credit card details directly on our server instead of proxying through Mobius/NMI's hosted tokenization

current_flow: 1. Frontend collects card info in Mobius-hosted iframe/form
2. Card data sent directly to Mobius/NMI (never touches our server)
3. Mobius returns a payment token or customer_vault_id
4. Our server uses token to charge via NMI API
proposed_flow: 1. Frontend collects card info in our own form
2. Card data POSTed to our server
3. Our server sends card data directly to NMI API
why_consider: Full control over checkout UX (no iframe styling limitations)
Can implement custom validation and error handling

pci_compliance_requirements.current_level: SAQ A or SAQ A-EP (card data never touches our servers)
pci_compliance_requirements.required_level: SAQ D or full PCI DSS assessment
key_requirements: Encrypt card data in transit (TLS 1.2+) - already done
Never store full card numbers (only last 4 + token)
Quarterly vulnerability scans by ASV (Approved Scanning Vendor)
Network segmentation for cardholder data environment

cost_estimate.ongoing_compliance: Significant engineering time for documentation and controls

implementation_steps: Determine if Mobius Pay allows direct API integration (vs requiring their hosted form)
Review NMI direct card submission API parameters (ccnumber, ccexp, cvv)
Implement server-side card validation (Luhn check, expiry, CVV format)
Add endpoint: POST /v1/checkout/card with {card_number, exp_month, exp_year, cvv, ...}
Ensure card data is never logged (scrub from request logs)
Ensure card data is never stored (only vault token after successful charge)
Pass PCI compliance assessment
Update frontend to use direct form instead of Mobius iframe
risks: PCI compliance burden and ongoing costs
Security liability if breached (card data exposure)
Mobius may not support direct integration (contractual requirement for hosted form)
More attack surface on our servers

recommendation.verdict: Probably not worth it for current scale
reasoning: PCI SAQ D compliance is expensive and time-consuming
Hosted tokenization (current approach) is industry standard
Mobius iframe can be styled reasonably well
Security risk/liability not worth UX improvement
reconsider_when: Processing >$1M/month and UX is measurably hurting conversion
Mobius hosted form has significant technical limitations
